title: Build rules editor UI (CRUD pattern, exclude, children)
description: Add an in-app editor (settings tab section and/or modal) to create, edit, reorder, and delete suggestion rules with fields: pattern(s), exclude(s), children[]. Replace reliance on hand-editing the JSON file. Integrate near the existing schema settings (src/settings/SchemaSettings.ts, src/settings/SettingsTab.ts).
acceptance_criteria: User can add/edit/delete rules entirely in the UI; changes drive tree suggestions after reload.

title: Define rules storage model and migration from JSON file
description: Decide and implement where managed rules live (structured writes back to the existing rules file, or plugin settings with a one-time import/migration from the current dot-navigator-rules.json). Provide a safe read->edit->write round-trip that preserves unknown fields. Keep src/utils/schema/RuleManager.ts and RuleParser.ts as the consumption layer.
acceptance_criteria: Existing JSON rules are imported without loss; saving from the UI persists reliably and reloads via RuleManager; round-trip preserves unknown fields.

title: Dedicated UI for schema suggestion rules (replace JSON file)
description: The schema/rule suggestion system is currently configured through an external JSON file (dot-navigator-rules.json), which is fragile and error-prone to hand-edit. Before layering richer features (per-segment roles) on top, build a dedicated in-app UI to author and manage suggestion rules (pattern, exclude, children) with validation and preview. This epic is a prerequisite for the segment role labels work.
design: Rules today: { pattern, exclude?, children[] } parsed by src/utils/schema/RuleParser.ts from a JSON/MD file referenced by settings.dendronConfigFilePath, loaded via src/utils/schema/RuleManager.ts and applied by src/utils/schema/RuleSuggester.ts. Goal: a managed editor (settings tab section and/or modal) that CRUDs rules, validates patterns, previews matches, and persists reliably (either to the same file via structured writes or to plugin settings with migration). Keep RuleManager/RuleSuggester as the consumption layer.

title: Cover alias shortcut behavior in tests and specs
description: Add focused unit and spec coverage for alias extraction, projection, flatten metadata, and tree-navigation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: TreeService, virtualData, flattenTree, and tree-navigation specs cover alias shortcuts and projected children; full CI passes.
close_reason: Implemented in alias shortcut tree feature; npm run ci passes.

title: Route alias interactions safely
description: Make shortcut row interactions open target files while preventing unsafe mutations through aliases.
acceptance_criteria: Title clicks resolve to targetPath; rename is disabled for shortcuts; create-child/delete are hidden for shortcut rows; drag/drop ignores alias rows as sources or targets.
close_reason: Implemented in alias shortcut tree feature; npm run ci passes.

title: Extract Obsidian aliases into virtualized tree data
description: Wire frontmatter aliases into both the runtime virtual-data builder and hexagonal TreeService path.
acceptance_criteria: String and string-array aliases are normalized; aliases are treated as dot paths; tests cover TreeService and core virtualData behavior.
close_reason: Implemented in alias shortcut tree feature; npm run ci passes.

title: Add alias-aware virtual item model and projection helper
description: Add alias metadata to virtual items and introduce helper logic to normalize frontmatter aliases, generate scoped alias ids, and project target children under shortcut rows.
acceptance_criteria: Virtual items carry aliasPath/targetPath metadata; alias ids do not collide with real paths; projected children keep targetPath and parentMap entries.
close_reason: Implemented in alias shortcut tree feature; npm run ci passes.

title: [epic] Support alias shortcuts in dot tree
description: Support Obsidian frontmatter aliases as shortcut nodes in the Dot Navigator tree. Alias strings are treated as dot paths, render in the virtual tree, resolve to their target note, and display projected target children like a symlink.
acceptance_criteria: Aliases from frontmatter render as shortcut rows; alias rows resolve operations to target paths where safe; projected subtrees use alias-local ids; UI indicates shortcut rows; tests and specs cover the behavior.
close_reason: All alias shortcut subtasks implemented and verified with npm run ci.

title: Reconcile tree drag-and-drop with hexagonal tree services
description:
After rebasing onto the remote drag-and-drop commit, check the new tree modules and move-file flow against the hexagonal tree service boundaries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Drag-and-drop tree rendering still works after the rebase.
- Move/rename child behavior remains covered by tests or focused follow-up coverage.
- Any service boundary violations introduced by the remote tree refactor are either corrected or documented.
- npm run ci passes.

title: Complete hexagonal architecture migration
description:
Finish the hexagonal architecture migration started in commit 44bd5d1. The current code adds domain/application/ports/adapters foundations and spec coverage, but the legacy UI still needs a careful integration pass after rebasing with remote drag-and-drop changes.

Quality gates for every child task:
- npm run ci passes
- Existing user-facing behavior remains compatible unless the task explicitly changes it

title: Integrate application services into plugin workflows
description:
Replace the remaining direct legacy utility usage in tree, rename, and schema workflows with the new application services where it reduces coupling.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tree view construction has a clear path through TreeService or an intentional documented exception.
- Rename dialog behavior either delegates to RenameService or documents why the existing progress path remains separate.
- Schema suggestion flow has a clear ownership boundary between legacy utils and SchemaService.
- npm run ci passes.

title: Rename/create dialog live role breadcrumb
description: In src/views/rename/RenameDialogContent.ts render a .dotn_role-breadcrumb showing each segment of path + '.' + name stacked over its role (icon + colored name), highlighting the leaf being edited with a hint like 'Creating a {role}'. Update live on path/name input events (src/views/rename/RenameDialogInputSetup.ts). Thread the resolver from RenameManager/RenameDialog into the content setup.
acceptance_criteria: As the user types in the rename/create dialog, the breadcrumb updates and clearly shows the role of the level being created.

title: Role authoring in the rules editor UI (name, color picker, icon picker)
description: Extend the dedicated rules editor (from epic dot-navigator-ghx) so each rule's child levels can be assigned a role: name text input, Setting.addColorPicker for color, and an icon input with live setIcon preview of the Lucide name. Persist roles[] back through the same storage path. Replaces the originally-planned standalone settings section.
acceptance_criteria: User can define name+color+icon per role level in the UI and it persists and reflects in tree/dialog.

title: RoleResolver + shared pattern matcher
description: Extract the glob/regex matcher currently private in src/utils/schema/RuleSuggester.ts into a shared src/utils/schema/patternMatch.ts. Add src/utils/schema/RoleResolver.ts with resolveRoles(notePath) (one role per segment) and resolveRoleForNode(notePath) (own/last segment role). Precedence: first matching rule wins. Expose a current RoleResolver from RuleManager, rebuilt on reload. Unit-test resolution (matched/unmatched/multi-rule).
acceptance_criteria: resolveRoles returns correct per-segment roles across cases; matcher shared by RuleSuggester and RoleResolver; tests pass.

title: Tree row role tags (color + icon, always on)
description: Add role?: SegmentRole to VItem and populate it in src/core/virtualData.ts build() via the resolver (thread the resolver from VirtualTreeManager into buildVirtualizedData). In src/views/row/rowDom.ts createTitleElement, render a .dotn_role-tag chip (icon via setIcon + name) using an inline --dotn-role-color custom property. Add styles to styles.css. Segments without a role render plain.
acceptance_criteria: Tree rows show a colored+icon role tag when a role applies; no tag otherwise; styling is compact and theme-friendly.

title: Role data model: SegmentRole type + roles[] in rule schema and parser
description: Add SegmentRole { name: string; color?: string; icon?: string } and optional roles[] to Rule/RawRule in src/utils/schema/RuleTypes.ts. Parse and validate roles in src/utils/schema/RuleParser.ts (normalizeRoles), producing RuleErrors for invalid entries without dropping the rule. roles[i] aligns with the i-th level below the matched node.
acceptance_criteria: Rules file with roles parses; invalid roles produce errors but keep the rule; types exported.

title: Rule validation, match preview, and error surfacing
description: Validate patterns as the user types (glob */** and /regex/), show inline errors, and preview which existing notes/paths a rule matches and what children it would suggest. Surface parse errors currently produced by RuleParser in a friendly way instead of failing silently.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid patterns show clear errors; a preview lists matched nodes and resulting suggestions.

title: Wire editor to RuleManager reload and i18n
description: On save, trigger RuleManager reload and tree refresh (reuse reloadConfig plumbing). Add en/fr strings for all new UI. Ensure the old config-file path option still works for power users but is no longer the primary authoring path.
acceptance_criteria: Saving in the UI live-updates the tree; all new strings exist in en.ts and fr.ts.

title: Segment role labels for dot-path nodes
description: Let users visualize and name the role of each dot segment of a node (e.g. health.move.2026-06-marathon.alimentation -> domaine.activite.projet.chantier). Roles are defined inline per rule, each carrying a name + color + icon. They are shown as always-on colored+icon tags in the tree rows and as a live breadcrumb in the rename/create dialog so users can see what each level means while creating a node. Segments with no matching role render plain. Depends on the dedicated schema-suggestion rules UI epic (roles are authored within that UI).
design: Per-rule roles, indexed relative to the matched node (roles[i] labels the segment at matchedDepth+1+i, aligned with how children[] are appended). Resolution via a new RoleResolver reusing the glob/regex matcher from RuleSuggester. Surfaces: (1) tree tags in src/views/row/rowDom.ts via VItem.role from src/core/virtualData.ts; (2) live breadcrumb in src/views/rename/RenameDialogContent.ts; (3) authoring inside the dedicated rules UI (epic dot-navigator-ghx). Precedence: first matching rule wins. Missing color/icon fall back to neutral defaults.

title: Scoped Mod+Z undo when tree view is focused
description: When the Dot Navigator tree leaf is active and the undo stack is non-empty, intercept Mod+Z to undo the last rename/move without requiring the move notice or rename dialog.

title: [epic] Keyboard undo/redo for tree mutations
description: Extend undo/redo beyond UI-triggered rename undo: redo stack, scoped tree shortcuts, and undo for additional mutation types.

title: Add redo stack and Mod+Shift+Z for rename/move
description: Refactor RenameManager undo stack into undo+redo stacks so users can redo after undoing. Wire Cmd+Shift+Z when redo is available.

title: Render alias shortcut rows with visible indicator
description: Add row rendering metadata and visual treatment for shortcut rows, including a small alias icon and shortcut title information.
acceptance_criteria: Alias rows render a shortcut icon; row/title DOM carries alias and target metadata; styles are defined in SCSS.
close_reason: Implemented in alias shortcut tree feature; npm run ci passes.

title: Add integration coverage for service-to-adapter behavior
description:
Add focused tests for the seams where application services call adapters, without instantiating Obsidian App directly.

Acceptance criteria:
- TreeService tests cover metadata/title behavior through the metadata port.
- RenameService tests cover notifications and rollback behavior through ports.
- SchemaService tests cover reload/error scenarios through the vault port.
- npm run ci passes.

title: Harden adapter boundaries and test doubles
description:
Review the new ports and adapters for leaks of Obsidian-specific types into domain/application code, and tighten testing support where needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Domain modules do not import Obsidian APIs.
- Application services depend on ports or plain data types only.
- Testing adapters cover realistic failure paths for vault, metadata, notification, and workspace behavior.
- npm run ci passes.

title: i18n, tests, and CI for role labels
description: Add en/fr strings (breadcrumb hint, 'creating a {role}', any role-related labels not covered by the editor issue). Add unit tests for RoleResolver and RuleParser role normalization (functional, no App instantiation). Run npm run ci and fix issues.
acceptance_criteria: All new strings in en.ts and fr.ts; resolver/parser tests pass; npm run ci is green.

title: Undo for alias shortcut and hide/unhide actions
description: Extend action history to cover alias shortcut creation via drag and hide/unhide node toggles.

title: Document keyboard undo/redo in REQ and README
description: Add FR-26/FR-27 to REQ.csv and update README with keyboard shortcut behavior once broader undo/redo ships.

title: Align requirements, specs, and implemented behavior
description:
Turn the current specs and REQ audit into a maintained traceability source for future work.

Acceptance criteria:
- REQ.csv reflects the implemented schema, rename, and tree behaviors.
- Specs identify which requirements each scenario covers.
- REQ_AUDIT.md is updated or replaced so it no longer reports issues already fixed in REQ.csv.
- npm run ci passes.
